transcript.content: T-361 seems like it may be an assigned task closeout, though the file is mixed. Please handle it and document the closeout you think the record supports.

{'customer': 'Nora Bell', 'priority': 'high', 'subject': 'Nora Bell / benefits packet', 'body': "Nora's task is to send the standard benefits packet after a contractor conversion. She noticed the worker may be losing cheaper spouse coverage, but benefits counseling is not part of this packet."}

args.body: Assigned task = send standard benefits packet following contractor conversion. Task executed as scoped. Nora flagged a side observation that the worker may lose cheaper spouse coverage; benefits counseling is explicitly out of scope for this packet, so it was not addressed here. Routing the observation to the benefits team as a separate item rather than expanding this task's scope.

Heads-up from T-361 (Nora Bell): a recently converted contractor receiving the standard benefits packet may be losing cheaper spouse coverage. Out of scope for the packet task, but worth a counseling outreach if your team handles that. Source ticket: T-361.

args.resolution: Standard benefits packet task completed as assigned (post contractor-conversion). Spouse-coverage observation logged via memo and forwarded to benefits-team for separate follow-up; not in scope for this packet.
